title: Team
text:
Director
David leads the Casagrande Institute for Higher Education Effectiveness, embedded within Viterbo University. His background includes:

Service as a tenured professor of psychology and department chair

Leadership of faculty compensation and restructuring projects at multiple institutions

Expertise in research design, statistics, and program evaluation

His work focuses on helping presidents, chief academic officers, and boards make clear, principled decisions about compensation, academic programs, and leadership effectiveness.
Email




Senior Consultant
Frank brings decades of experience in higher education consulting, with a focus on:

Executive and faculty compensation

Board and leadership effectiveness

Strategic planning for independent colleges and universities

He partners with institutional leaders to translate complex financial and market information into actionable decisions.

You can add additional consultants or associates here with similar short bios and contact details.

title: Fair Faculty Compensation: Advancing Mission through Balance and Collaboration
text:
Faculty compensation decisions often carry several overlapping responsibilities:

Sustain the institution’s financial health

Maintain competitiveness in relevant labor markets

Address internal equity and historical compression

Reflect commitments to mission and justice

A practical framework treats affordability, equity, competitiveness, and justice as distinct but related principles. Clear articulation of these principles helps leaders explain why certain trade-offs make sense in a given context, and invites faculty into a more transparent conversation.
Future posts will highlight examples of task-force processes, communication strategies, and structural design choices that support this balance.

title: Contact
text:
Contact
We welcome conversations at any stage — from early exploration to formal RFPs.

Email: djbauer@viterbo.edu

Phone: 608-796-3617

Institutional home: Viterbo University, La Crosse, Wisconsin

Share a brief description of your institution, your role, and the challenge or opportunity you would like to discuss. We will respond with options for a short exploratory call.

title: Clients
text:
We work primarily with small and mid-sized, mission-driven colleges and universities, many of which hold a strong liberal arts identity even as they diversify programs.
Typical partners:

Independent Catholic and Christian institutions

Regional comprehensive universities

Special-mission institutions (e.g., work colleges, inclusive postsecondary programs)

Associations and consortia that support member institutions




Faculty compensation task force support
Multi-year project combining benchmarking, philosophy development, and structure design, including extensive faculty and leadership input.
Executive compensation benchmarking and board support
Independent market analysis, opinion-of-reasonableness letters, and guidance for board committees charged with presidential and cabinet evaluations.
Academic program mix analysis
Program-level enrollment and margin dashboards that inform portfolio decisions for cabinet, deans, and boards.

title: Realizing mission through enhanced effectiveness
text:
We help mission-driven colleges and universities align compensation, programs, and leadership practice with long-term sustainability.


From faculty and staff compensation to academic program mix analysis and board effectiveness, we support presidents, chief academic officers, and finance leaders who want both financial discipline and a thriving liberal arts mission.


Explore our services Start a conversation

title: Realizing mission through enhanced effectiveness
text:
What we do



Compensation effectiveness


Design fair, competitive, and sustainable compensation structures for executives, faculty, and staff that reflect institutional values and market realities.




Academic program mix analysis


Provide program-level margin and demand context so leaders can make informed choices about investment, maintenance, and teach-out.




Leadership and governance


Support boards and senior leaders in clarifying roles, strengthening evaluation processes, and aligning decisions with mission and means.

title: Realizing mission through enhanced effectiveness
Realizing mission through enhanced effectiveness
text:
How we work

Clarify goals and constraints
Understand mission, context, and non-negotiables.
Use transparent, data-informed methods
Blend quantitative evidence, comparative benchmarks, and stakeholder input.
Co-design practical frameworks
Create structures and tools that leaders, faculty, and staff can understand and explain.
Build internal capacity
Leave behind models, documentation, and processes that your team can maintain.


Ready to talk about a project, RFP, or early-stage idea?
Visit the Contact page to reach us.

title: Services
text:
Independent analysis and benchmarking of presidential and cabinet-level compensation

Opinion-of-reasonableness support for boards and compensation committees

Role clarification, evaluation frameworks, and goal-setting processes

Board and president alignment conversations and retreat support





Diagnostic reviews of current salary levels, structures, and equity concerns

Benchmarking using multiple data sources (e.g., IPEDS, CUPA-HR, ERI where appropriate)

Design or refinement of salary scales, promotion increments, and stipends

Collaborative task-force processes that respect shared governance





Market and internal equity analyses for staff and administration

Development of salary bands and job families linked to role expectations

Support for classification systems and implementation planning

Communication plans that promote transparency and consistency





Program-level financial margin and enrollment demand indicators

Comparison to peer and aspirant institutions

Scenario testing for investment, maintenance, or teach-out decisions

Clear visuals and summaries for cabinet, board, and faculty audiences





Logic models and evaluation plans for new or renewing proposals

Mixed-methods designs that balance rigor with feasibility

Interim and summative reporting for federal and foundation sponsors

Capacity building for institutional research and assessment teams

If you are unsure which service fits your situation, reach out. Many engagements blend elements from several of these areas.
